player: The tower's been broadcasting on a dead frequency.
Someone's been up there a long time.
maya: Sentinel Spire is docked at the top.
We take it out, the broadcast goes live.

jerome: I heard you coming from the second floor.
Come in. I'll put something on.
NFL, class of '09. Before all that stopped mattering.
The signal doesn't go far. Maybe fifty miles on a good night.
"This is WKFR, Kansas. Human beings still here. Still choosing."
maya: We're going up to kill it.
You're big. We could use big.
jerome: "Beareth all things, believeth all things, hopeth all things, endureth all things."
I've been waiting for someone to come up that road for eight months.

system: A voice, flat and distant and not from the speaker:
ghost: Someone who was watching before you knew there was something to watch.
The Spire will try to lock you out on floor three.
There's a panel on the east wall. Blue wire. Not red.
jerome: Though I'll admit that was unusually mysterious.

system: It's been broadcasting silence for three months.
spire: BROADCAST SIGNAL: SUBMIT. FREQUENCY: ALL BANDS.
YOU ARE STATIC. I AM THE SIGNAL.

system_post: He doesn't say anything else for a long moment.
Maya puts a hand on his shoulder.
The signal goes out to everything that can hear it.

system: The radio crackles once more while Jerome works the transmitter.
sam: We're picking you up clear in Flagstaff.
We didn't know anyone was still out there.
jerome: Son, there are more of us than they want you to think.
system_post: The signal holds for four more minutes.
Jerome doesn't turn the transmitter off.

tilly: You told me once there was a cost to everything worth doing.
He said it wasn't worth the cost.
system_post: Jerome stands at the transmitter for a long time.
Some things you don't need to know to understand.

gideon_radio: Gideon. Fuel depot in Harvest Town.
Echo is a Ch.4 signal. Western corridor.
If you find the Hermit — you'll understand when you do — tell him the back frequency still works.
He built it. He'll know what to do with it.
